name: Old Changi Hospital
body: Built by the British in the 1930s, the hilltop complex became, during the Japanese Occupation, a site associated with the Kempeitai and with the suffering of those held and interrogated there. Long after it closed as a hospital, it kept its reputation as one of the most haunted places on the island.

The accounts repeat the same notes: cold pockets in warm corridors, the cries of children in empty wards, a nurse seen drifting where no one walks, faces at the windows photographed from outside. Trespassers describe the building seeming to wake around them — the sense of being counted, and found.
note: Distinguish layers here: documented wartime history, decades of trespasser lore, and the embellishment of ghost-tour retellings. The strongest entries note which layer each claim belongs to.
